# Firma.dev Review

Last modified: |  Fred Magni

Ever felt the pain of integrating e-signatures into your app? The endless contracts, opaque pricing, and clunky SDKs? Say hello to Firma.dev, the electronic signature API built from the ground up for developers. It’s not just another e-signature solution; it’s a game-changer, offering robust capabilities at an unbelievably low price point – averaging just €0.029 per envelope.

Firma.dev Review

Visit Website  Have an AI Tool? Submit it here! [...] Ultra-Low Pricing: At just €0.029 per envelope, Firma.dev is arguably the cheapest e-signature API on the market. No subscriptions, no minimums, no contracts – pay only for what you use.
 Developer-First Integration: A complete REST API, detailed Swagger/OpenAPI docs, pre-built UI components, and webhook events ensure rapid integration across any programming language or framework (JS, React, Vue, Angular, mobile).
 Fully White-Labeled: Maintain your brand integrity by embedding e-signatures that look and feel like they’re native to your application. Rebrand and resell with ease, turning e-signatures into a new revenue stream. [...] ## Main Target

Firma.dev is engineered specifically for SaaS platforms and developers who need to integrate e-signature capabilities efficiently and affordably. If you’re building an application and want to offer turnkey document signing without the usual vendor lock-in or exorbitant costs, Firma.dev is your go-to. It’s perfect for companies looking to manage unlimited teams, white-label the entire experience, and scale effortlessly.

| Key Aspect | Firma.dev Offering |
 --- |
| Cost per Envelope | €0.029 (approx. 3¢) |
| Key Compliances | GDPR, eIDAS, SOC 2, ISO 27001 |

## Top Alternatives to Firma.dev

Let’s explore and discover the best alternatives and similar tools to Firma.dev, carefully selected and ranked based on functionality, reliability, and user experience.

results.title: DocuSign Alternatives for Developers: API-First Options That Won't Drain Your Budget | Firma.dev Blog
results.content:
The limitations show up in embedded and white-label scenarios. SignWell works well for applications where redirecting users to a signing page is acceptable, but the branding and layout customization options are more limited than purpose-built embedded solutions. If you're building a SaaS product where the signing experience needs to feel native to your application, you may find the customization ceiling frustrating.

For simpler use cases with moderate volume, SignWell offers a reasonable middle ground between enterprise pricing and full API flexibility.

1. ### PandaDoc [...] The developer experience is polished. SDKs exist for popular languages, the REST API follows predictable conventions, and the test environment lets you build without commitment. Embedded signing works, and there's decent branding customization available.

The Dropbox acquisition brought integration benefits for teams already in that ecosystem but also introduced some enterprise-oriented complexity. Pricing tiers have shifted over time, and API access sits separate from the consumer product plans. For teams sending high volumes, the per-request economics still favor pay-as-you-go alternatives. [...] The difference at scale is dramatic. At 1,000 envelopes per month, Firma.dev costs €29. DocuSign's comparable tier runs €300-480 plus potential overages. At 10,000 envelopes, you're looking at €290 versus custom enterprise pricing that typically runs into thousands.

Beyond pricing, Firma.dev was built specifically for developers embedding signatures into applications. The API includes both an embedded template editor and signing editor, so you can build complete document workflows without redirecting users to external pages.

results.title: Firma.dev API v1.6, v1.7, and v1.8: Split PDFs, Hand-Drawn Signatures, and Custom Email Templates | Firma.dev Blog
results.content:
Note that v1.8 also corrected several field names in the OpenAPI spec to match the actual API responses. If your integration already handles the real response fields (which it almost certainly does), no changes are needed.

Full v1.8 changelog

## Upgrading

All three releases are fully backward compatible. No breaking changes, no migration steps required. You can upgrade your API version from v1.5 straight to v1.8 and everything will continue working as before, with the new features available immediately.

For the full changelog with every schema change and migration note, check the API Changelog in the docs. If you're just getting started with Firma.dev, you can get your API key for free and start building today.

Previous release notes: v1.5 | v1.3 and v1.4

1. Heading [...] Both URLs are pre-signed and expire after 1 hour. If you need a fresh link, just fetch the signing request again. This is particularly useful if you need to store the signed document in one system and the audit trail in another, or if your end users only need the clean signed PDF without the certificate pages appended.

v1.6 also cleaned up field type naming. The API now accepts both `initials` and `initial`, as well as `textarea` and `text_area`, and normalizes them automatically. If your integration was already using the original names, nothing breaks. If you prefer the normalized forms, those work too.

Full v1.6 changelog

results.title: Multi-Language Signature Support | Firma.dev
results.content:
Your signers type their names in Japanese, Russian, Greek, Korean, or any Latin-based language. We renders them beautifully with curated signature fonts. No broken characters. No fallback fonts. Just signatures that look right.

## Supported Writing Systems

Firma.dev automatically detects and renders signatures in 5 script families:

Russian, Ukrainian, Bulgarian, Serbian

Greek

Modern Greek (including ά, έ, ί, ό and other accented characters)

Zen Kurenaido [...] If auto-detection gets it wrong (rare, but possible with mixed-script names), signers can manually switch scripts in the signing UI.

## Why This Matters for Global SaaS

Three user profile icons with name tags in purple, gold, and blue, featuring 

### Global Name Rendering

If you're building software for international markets, your users expect their names to display correctly. A Russian name rendered in a Latin fallback font looks broken. A Japanese name that shows as rectangles signals a low-quality product.

### Universal Script Support

results.title: Legal Requirements for Electronic Signatures: A Developer's Checklist | Firma.dev Blog
results.content:
Fix: Log everything listed in the audit trail section above. Make logs immutable.

Broken record retention

Documents were stored, but links expired or files got deleted.

Fix: Implement retention policies aligned with document type. Contracts typically need 7+ years. Never auto-delete without explicit policy.

Unclear signer identity

Anyone with the link could have signed. No verification that the named signer was the actual person.

Fix: Require email verification at minimum. Add SMS codes, SSO, or knowledge-based authentication for higher-stakes documents.

Editable documents after signing

The signed document can be modified without detection.

Fix: Hash documents at signing time. Store the hash in your audit log. Verify hash on retrieval.

Pre-checked consent boxes [...] Pre-checked consent boxes

Consent was "captured" but the user never actively opted in.

Fix: Require affirmative action. Unchecked boxes that the user must check, or explicit "I agree" buttons.

Missing timestamp or wrong timezone

You recorded "signed at 3:00 PM" but not which timezone.

Fix: Use ISO 8601 format with timezone offset, or store everything in UTC with the signer's timezone noted seperately.

## How Firma.dev Handles Compliance for You

Building all of this from scratch takes time. Firma.dev handles the compliance infrastructure so you can focus on your application.

Built-in audit trails: Every signature event generates an immutable record with signer identity, timestamps, IP addresses, document hashes, and consent capture. Logs can't be modified or deleted. [...] Practical rule: If you build to AdES standards, you automatically satisfy US requirements and most international frameworks. The extra effort is minimal and the coverage is broader.

Data residency matters: EU users may require EU data storage for GDPR compliance. Know where your documents and audit logs are hosted.

## Common Mistakes That Invalidate E-Signatures

These are the bugs that get signatures thrown out in court:

Missing consent capture

The user signed, but you never recorded that they agreed to electronic transactions.

Fix: Add explicit consent step before first signature. Store the timestamp and what was disclosed.

No audit trail

You have the signed PDF but no record of who signed, when, or how.
